title: Understanding the Performance Review Process
text:
Q: What should I know about the performance review process, timeline, and how to complete a self-assessment?
A: The performance review process is an annual evaluation where employees receive feedback on their work and set goals for the upcoming year. Reviews typically occur in the last quarter, with specific dates communicated by your manager. The process includes a self-assessment, where you reflect on your achievements and areas for improvement. To complete your self-assessment, review your goals from the past year and provide examples of how you met or exceeded them. Be honest about challenges and consider how you can address them moving forward. This is also an opportunity to express your career aspirations and any support you might need. Completing your self-assessment thoroughly can lead to a more productive discussion during your review.
